title: GW Methods for Graph Matching
description: This project evaluates two frameworks for node matching in complex networks: Gromov-Wasserstein Learning (GWL) and Bregman Alternating Projected Gradient (BAPG). It explores the trade-offs between computational speed and matching accuracy in noisy environments.

title: Lottery Ticket Analysis
description: The project investigates the Lottery Ticket Hypothesis for neural networks, focusing on convolutional neural networks trained on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100, and explore how lottery tickets transfer across datasets and how ticket sparsity relates to the intrinsic dimensionality of the target function.

title: Body Region Matching with High Density Point Clouds
description: A real-time motion capture system that uses camera-based tracking, IR/LED markers, and calibration pipelines (built with ROS, Python, and MATLAB) to accurately reconstruct 3D positions for objects or markers.

title: Cancer Identification using Topologyal Data Analysis
description: The research aims to determine how topological methods, which focus on the 'shape' of data rather than specific coordinates, can extract noise-tolerant insights from complex biological datasets like 10x Visium Spatial Data.

title: Wasserstein Correspondence Study
description: This research evaluates optimal transport frameworks for matching point clouds in motion capture (mocap) sequences, specifically addressing frames separated by large time intervals.

title: Misleading Narratives in Data
description: This project explores how data visualization choices can shape and even manipulate interpretation of the same dataset about complaints and exonerations related to race. It presents two contrasting visualizations: one arguing that complaint processing is fair and unbiased, and another showing how selective metrics can create a misleading narrative of racial disparity.

title: Bike Mapping and Map Visualizations
description: A website using data for bike repair and bike route mapping. Showing a combination of geolocational data and frequency overlays to show most often used areas.
